2604.27616.title: RoadMapper: A Multi-Agent System for Roadmap Generation of Solving Complex Research Problems
2604.27616.paper_summary: People commonly leverage structured content to accelerate knowledge acquisition and research problem solving. Among these, roadmaps guide researchers through hierarchical subtasks to solve complex research problems step by step. Despite progress in structured content generation, the roadmap generation task has remained unexplored. To bridge this gap, we introduce RoadMap, a novel benchmark designed to evaluate the ability of large language models (LLMs) to construct high-quality roadmaps for solving complex research problems. Based on this, we identify three limitations of LLMs: (1) lack of professional knowledge, (2) unreasonable task decomposition, and (3) disordered logical relationships. To address these challenges, we propose RoadMapper, an LLM-based multi-agent system that decomposes the research roadmap generation task into three key stages (i.e., initial generation, knowledge augmentation, and iterative "critique-revise-evaluate"). Extensive experiments demonstrate that RoadMapper can improve LLMs' ability for roadmap generation, while enhancing average performance by more than 8% and saving 84% of the time required by human experts, highlighting its effectiveness and application potential.
2604.27616.paper_summary_zh: 人們通常利用結構化內容來加速知識獲取和研究問題解決。<lb/>在這些內容中，路線圖引導研究人員通過分層的子任務逐步解決複雜的研究問題。<lb/>儘管在結構化內容生成方面取得了進展，路線圖生成任務仍然未被探索。<lb/>為了填補這一空白，我們介紹了RoadMap，一個旨在評估大型語言模型（LLMs）構建高質量路線圖以解決複雜研究問題能力的新基準。<lb/>基於此，我們識別出LLMs的三個限制：（1）缺乏專業知識，（2）不合理的任務分解，以及（3）混亂的邏輯關係。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了RoadMapper，一個基於LLM的多代理系統，將研究路線圖生成任務分解為三個關鍵階段（即，初始生成、知識增強和迭代的「批評-修訂-評估」）。<lb/>大量實驗表明，RoadMapper可以提高LLMs的路線圖生成能力，同時平均性能提升超過8%，並節省人類專家所需的84%時間，突顯了其有效性和應用潛力。
2604.27616.author: Jiacheng Liu et.al.
2604.27616.authors: Jiacheng Liu, Zichen Tang, Zhongjun Yang, Xinyi Hu, Xueyuan Lin, Linwei Jia, Ruofei Bai, Rongjin Li, Shiyao Peng, Haocheng Gao, Haihong E
